title: Request for hearing in another state
violation_description: SC Court should have requested TN to hold hearing before ordering mother to give up child in Tennessee while she had pending action

title: Jurisdiction priority
violation_description: Jurisdiction question not given priority on calendar and not handled expeditiously

title: Notice and proof of service
violation_description: No proof of service; no service as required under TN law; no right for judge to continue hearing she lacked authority to hear
who_violated: SC Court / Judge Crouch / Emily Hinson

title: Immunity
violation_description: Emily had to serve Plaintiff at hearing July 15 because she acknowledged Plaintiff was under TN jurisdiction - Sawyer was too when taken
evidence: Service at hearing acknowledges TN jurisdiction

title: Communication with out-of-state courts
violation_description: SC Court required to allow all parties to argue facts of jurisdiction BEFORE decision was made - this was not done
evidence: No jurisdiction hearing before custody change

title: Testimony of out-of-state witnesses
violation_description: SC should have obtained testimony from TN daycare director and family members by phone/electronic means - Crouch had heard matter before April 11

title: Preservation of records
violation_description: SC should have preserved records until AFTER TN held hearing for Order of Protection or AFTER UCCJEA jurisdiction hearing
evidence: Records not preserved for TN proceedings

title: Basis for jurisdiction
violation_description: SC did not meet grounds for custody determination - child gone 6 months with no PARENT living in SC; TN did NOT decline jurisdiction; TN met criteria
evidence: No parent residing in SC; child in TN 6+ months

title: Exclusive continuing jurisdiction
violation_description: Court did not determine that no other state had jurisdiction or acknowledge REQUIREMENT to make determination; TN court determined Mother and Child reside in TN
evidence: TN court found Mother and Child in TN

title: Custody modification by another state
violation_description: TN did NOT determine they lacked jurisdiction; TN ordered SC to hold evidentiary hearing to determine jurisdiction which SC did not do
evidence: TN ordered evidentiary hearing - SC ignored

title: Temporary jurisdiction
violation_description: SC cannot make final custody determination UNTIL state becomes HOME STATE; Crouch did not specify time period for other state to obtain order; Newton did specify; Crouch required to IMMEDIATELY communicate with TN upon learning of custody action
evidence: No time period specified; no communication with TN court

title: Notice obligation
violation_description: Notice not given as required by TN law; notice required for Pete and Jane too

title: Stays of other proceedings
violation_description: SC Court lacked authority to exercise jurisdiction upon learning at commencement that custody determination was pending in TN; should have ordered Stay and communicated with TN

title: Inconvenient forum
violation_description: Jurisdiction challengeable at any time; evidence and child testimony in TN; SC should have found itself inconvenient and requested TN hold child custody proceeding; TN is main jurisdiction for child and mother safety
evidence: Child and evidence in TN; domestic violence factors favor TN

title: Unjustifiable jurisdiction
violation_description: Kent his parents GAL and attorneys engaged in unjustifiable conduct - DV threats harassment fraud legal abuse financial abuse child neglect fraud upon court
who_violated: Kent Cales; Jane McManus; Pete McManus; Emily Hinson; Joshua Pozsik
evidence: Pattern of unjustifiable conduct to create SC jurisdiction

title: Information regarding other proceedings
violation_description: First pleading must provide child address and residency for last 5 years plus any other custody proceeding in another court - this was not properly disclosed
evidence: TN proceeding not disclosed in pleadings
